# Pinpoint 311

> Free, open-source municipal reporting software for local governments.

## What is Pinpoint 311?

Pinpoint 311 is a free, open-source (MIT license) 311 system that lets residents report infrastructure issues (potholes, streetlights, graffiti, etc.) and gives municipal staff AI-powered tools to triage and resolve them. It replaces expensive commercial platforms like SeeClickFix ($5-20K/year) and outdated email/phone intake systems.

## Key Features

- Resident portal: 60-second submissions, no account needed, 109 languages
- Staff dashboard: AI triage via Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, priority scoring, internal notes
- Admin console: white-label branding, GeoJSON map layers, service routing rules
- AI capabilities: photo analysis, multilingual translation, conversational analytics, PII auto-redaction
- Geospatial: boundary enforcement, asset selection, hotspot detection, proximity analysis
- Notifications: email and SMS status updates
- Research tools: 60+ field CSV/GeoJSON export with census integration
- Open311 v2 API compliant
- Runs on a $5/month server (~160MB memory footprint)

## Links

- Homepage: https://pinpoint311.org
- GitHub: https://github.com/Pinpoint-311/Pinpoint-311
- Live Demo: https://demo.pinpoint311.org
- Get Started: https://pinpoint311.org/get-started.html
- About: https://pinpoint311.org/about.html

## Technical Stack

- Backend: Python, FastAPI, Celery, PostgreSQL/PostGIS
- Frontend: React, TypeScript, Vite
- AI: Google Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite (vision + text)
- Maps: Google Maps API
- Deployment: Docker Compose, Caddy reverse proxy

## Contact

- GitHub Issues: https://github.com/Pinpoint-311/Pinpoint-311/issues
- License: MIT (no vendor lock-in)
- Fiscal sponsor: Hack Club (501(c)(3) nonprofit)
